humanSummary.text:
Correctness audit of UTSUSHI-027 at commit d78928f. The single feat commit (3478 +) adds `utsushi_core::conformance::trace_branch` (trace.rs, branch.rs, fixtures.rs, mod.rs), 12 new stable semantic codes appended to `conformance::diagnostics::codes::ALL`, and a fixture tree under `crates/utsushi-core/tests/fixtures/conformance/trace_branch/` with 3 positive + 11 negative JSON fixtures plus a `tests/conformance_trace_branch.rs` integration suite.

Verification (worktree, direnv):
  - `cargo clean -p utsushi-core` then `cargo test -p utsushi-core --no-run`: tests build clean (8 test binaries). (A stale-build flake was observed without the clean step; from a clean baseline the build succeeds.)
  - `cargo test -p utsushi-core`: 356 passed / 0 failed / 5 ignored (lib) + 10 + 9 + 14 + 15 + 11 + 3 + 16 = 78 across 7 test binaries, all green. The new `conformance_trace_branch` integration binary contributes 14 passed; the 61 new lib unit tests (matched against the worker's 61 + 14 = 75 count) live inside `conformance::trace_branch::{trace,branch,fixtures,mod}::tests`.
  - `cargo test -p utsushi-fixture`: 0 tests / 0 failures (utsushi-fixture has no test surface in this slice).
  - `cargo clippy -p utsushi-core --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean.
  - `cargo fmt --check`: clean.
  - `just check`: FAILS at the `pnpm exec vp check` step with `ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_EXEC_FIRST_FAIL` / `Command "vp" not found`. This is an environment / tooling defect on the audit host (the `vp` binary is not on PATH inside the worktree's pnpm exec), NOT an UTSUSHI-027 regression: `vp` is a pre-existing repo tool and the failure happens before any UTSUSHI-027 file is touched. Not raised as a finding against this node; tracked under the existing tooling backlog.

Greps for engine leakage and process boundary discipline:
  - `grep -rn 'Command::new' crates/utsushi-core/src/conformance/trace_branch/`: no hits. The check substrate stays in-process; no subprocess spawn.
  - `grep -rniE 'xp3|kirikiri|kag|rgss|rpgmaker|reallive|siglus' crates/utsushi-core/src/conformance/trace_branch/`: three matches, all in trace.rs doc-comments about `bridge_unit_id`, NONE referencing engine identifiers; the substring matches are coincidental ("bridge" in "Bridge unit linkage"). Engine-neutral confirmed.
  - `grep` on codes::ALL (crates/utsushi-core/src/conformance/diagnostics.rs lines 45-95) confirms all 12 new constants (TRACE_TEXT_MISMATCH, TRACE_ORDER_MISMATCH, TRACE_SPEAKER_MISMATCH, TRACE_EVENT_MISSING, TRACE_EVENT_UNEXPECTED, BRIDGE_UNIT_UNLINKED, BRIDGE_UNIT_DIVERGENT, BRANCH_MISSING, BRANCH_UNEXPECTED, BRANCH_CHOICE_PATH_MISMATCH, BRANCH_OUTCOME_MISMATCH, TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM) are members of `ALL` and the unit test `new_codes_have_no_duplicates_in_codes_all` (mod.rs:138) enforces uniqueness.

Acceptance-criteria walk-through:
  1. "Intentional text, order, branch, and bridge-unit mismatches fail predictably." Confirmed via `negative_*_fixture_fails_with_*` integration tests (14/14 pass with the asserted mismatch kinds). The trace pairing loop (trace.rs lines 176-292) emits every per-event mismatch in the fixed evaluation order plan §3.3.1 specified (BridgeUnitUnlinked / BridgeUnitDivergent / OrderShift / TextDifference / SpeakerMismatch), never short-circuiting inside a single event — see `trace_check_run_collects_all_per_event_mismatches_not_only_the_first()` in trace.rs::tests. Branch pairing (branch.rs lines 127-209) is id-keyed via HashMap join, emitting Missing + Unexpected + ChoicePathDivergent + OutcomeDifference exhaustively.
  2. "Trace and branch failures identify expected + observed runtime event ids." Confirmed: `TraceMismatch.{expected_event_id, observed_event_id}` and `BranchMismatch.{expected_branch_id, observed_branch_id}` are populated on every emission; `observed_event_id = None` is reachable only for `TraceMismatchKind::Missing` (observed trace shorter than golden) per plan §3.3.1, and `observed_branch_id = None` only for `BranchMismatchKind::Missing`. Sentinel strings `BEYOND_GOLDEN_SENTINEL = "<beyond-golden>"` (trace.rs:22) and `UNKNOWN_GOLDEN_SENTINEL = "<unknown-golden>"` (branch.rs:20) are exported constants, asserted by `trace_check_unexpected_mismatch_uses_documented_sentinel_event_id` (trace.rs::tests line ~969).
  3. "Trace-only conformance remains distinct from screenshot/render conformance." Confirmed structurally and behaviorally: `accepts_text_trace_evidence` (trace.rs:506) and `accepts_branch_capture_evidence` (branch.rs:409) both reject `EvidenceRef::FrameArtifactRef` and `RuntimeArtifactKind::{Screenshot, FrameCapture, Recording}`. `TraceCheckResult::into_conformance_result` checks the tier ceiling at line 328-335 and runs each evidence ref through the filter at lines 338-344, 351-357. `trace_into_conformance_result_rejects_frame_artifact_evidence` and `..._rejects_recording_runtime_artifact` are present in trace.rs::tests; analogous tests exist for branch.

Hard-constraint walk-through:
  - **Trace E1 ceiling.** Enforced in `into_conformance_result` for both result types against `ProfileId::{TextTrace,BranchCapture}.evidence_tier_ceiling()` (which UTSUSHI-026 binds to E1).
  - **Engine-neutral.** Confirmed — no engine identifiers in trace_branch/ or fixtures.
  - **No optionality.** Honoured. `BranchCheckOptions.replay_log_run_id: Option<String>` is the only optional field; the worker's deviation note about validating it in `BranchConformanceCheck::new()` is confirmed at branch.rs:107-109 (when Some, validates via the shared `super::trace::validate_id_field`). Plan §13.5 explicitly carves this out as evidence-emission-only.
  - **12 stable codes registered.** Confirmed (see codes::ALL section above).
  - **Exhaustive collection.** The comparison loops never short-circuit; per-event detail is capped at 256 bytes via `TRACE_MISMATCH_DETAIL_BYTE_CAP` (trace.rs:28), reused by branch via `super::trace::TRACE_MISMATCH_DETAIL_BYTE_CAP`. `trace_mismatch_detail_truncates_at_256_bytes()` and `trace_mismatch_detail_rejects_local_path_substring()` cover the cap + redaction.

Deviations spot-check:
  - `BranchCheckOptions.replay_log_run_id` validated in `new()`: confirmed.
  - Branch Pass evidence uses `branch_id` as `bridge_unit_id`: confirmed at branch.rs:230-234 — `EvidenceRef::BridgeUnit { bridge_unit_id: branch.branch_id.clone() }`. Recorded as F002 below.
  - `TextNormalisation` derives `Default`: confirmed (trace.rs); clippy-clean.

Findings: P0=0, P1=0, P2=0, P3=4 (3 worker-pre-identified, 1 new on the trace-overclaim diagnostic surface). completionDecision=complete_allowed.

findings.title: MaxMismatchesPerEvent truncation cap deferred (worker pre-identified)
findings.description: Worker-pre-identified. Plan §13.3 explicitly defers a `MaxMismatchesPerEvent` truncation cap to a future tightening. The current substrate collects every per-event mismatch unbounded; with `TRACE_MISMATCH_DETAIL_BYTE_CAP = 256` and at most 5 mismatch kinds per event (BridgeUnitUnlinked / BridgeUnitDivergent / OrderShift / TextDifference / SpeakerMismatch), the worst-case output is bounded at `golden_len * 5 * 256 ≈ golden_len * 1.3 KB`. For the alpha track's ≤5-event fixtures this is well within budget, but a production engine port that emits a 10k-event trace with every event divergent would emit ~13 MB of mismatch text. The brief's hard constraint of "exhaustive collection (no truncation/short-circuit other than 256-byte detail cap)" is honoured today; this is preserved as a future-work tracker for when a real engine port is the producer.

evidence.detail: Plan §13.3 records the deferred cap explicitly.
Plan §14 Out of Scope lists `Per-event MaxMismatchesPerEvent truncation cap` as future work.
TraceConformanceCheck::run collects every kind per event with no per-event cap; only the detail string is truncated.

findings.impact: Negligible while UTSUSHI-027 ships with synthetic fixtures only. Surface area widens once UTSUSHI-031+ engine ports produce real traces against the check; a large divergence then balloons mismatch output to megabytes. The current contract (exhaustive collection) is the right default for the alpha track and matches the brief; the cap is a production-readiness knob.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: `TraceCheckOptions` gains a `max_mismatches_per_event: Option<NonZeroU32>` (None = unbounded, today's default) so the wire-shape default stays exhaustive.

When set, the per-event loop emits at most N mismatches per event and appends a single typed `TraceMismatchKind::TruncatedPerEvent` sentinel mismatch with the count of suppressed kinds so the truncation is observable.
A new stable code `utsushi.conformance.trace_per_event_truncated` is added to `codes::ALL` and to the round-trip parity test.
Tests cover the default (unbounded) path and the bounded path with N=1 + N=3 on a synthetic 4-divergence event.

proposedDagNode.title: MaxMismatchesPerEvent truncation cap for trace conformance
proposedDagNode.summary: Add an opt-in per-event truncation cap to `TraceCheckOptions` so production engine-port runs against large traces emit bounded mismatch lists without losing the exhaustive default that UTSUSHI-027's alpha-track fixtures rely on.
deliverables: `TraceMismatchKind::TruncatedPerEvent` typed sentinel that carries the suppressed-count detail
Unit + integration tests for the unbounded and bounded paths
acceptanceCriteria: Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = None` reproduces the current exhaustive emission (regression-tested).
Setting `max_mismatches_per_event = Some(N)` caps per-event emissions at N and appends exactly one `TruncatedPerEvent` sentinel when suppression occurred.
The new code is in `codes::ALL` and the mod.rs parity test exhaustively covers it.
auditFocus: Truncation observability via the typed sentinel kind
Codes-registry parity for the new code

findings.title: Bridge-unit id normalisation deferred (worker pre-identified)
findings.description: Worker-pre-identified. Plan §13.1 explicitly defers a normalised bridge-unit-id comparison mode (e.g. leading-zero stripping, lowercase folding) to a future addition. The check today compares `bridge_unit_id` strings byte-for-byte; an engine adapter that legitimately emits the same UUIDv7 with different surface forms (e.g. uppercase hex vs lowercase) would trip a spurious `BridgeUnitDivergent` mismatch. The plan accepts this as the correct default: stability of the id surface is UTSUSHI-031+ engine-port contract, not a UTSUSHI-027 concern. Recorded so the orchestrator carries the future-work tracker when the first engine port reveals a normalisation gap.

evidence.detail: Plan §13.1 records the deferred normalisation explicitly.
Plan §14 Out of Scope lists `Bridge-unit id normalisation` as future work.
BridgeUnitDivergent compares `observed_id != golden.bridge_unit_id` by byte equality.

findings.impact: Negligible while engine ports do not yet exist. Once a real adapter lowers UTSUSHI-022 `TextLine.bridge_ref` into `ObservedTextEvent.bridge_unit_id`, any surface-form drift trips a false mismatch that requires a per-adapter patch instead of a substrate option.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: `TraceCheckOptions` gains an opt-in `bridge_unit_id_normalisation: BridgeUnitIdNormalisation` enum with at least `Exact` (default, preserves current behaviour) and `LowercaseHex` (lowercase ASCII before equality).
Normalisation is applied symmetrically to golden + observed before the `BridgeUnitDivergent` check.
New unit test covers a fixture where golden uses lowercase hex and observed uses uppercase hex; `Exact` mode trips `BridgeUnitDivergent`, `LowercaseHex` mode passes.

proposedDagNode.title: Optional bridge-unit-id normalisation modes for trace conformance
proposedDagNode.summary: Add an opt-in normalisation mode to bridge-unit-id equality so engine adapters that emit stable UUIDs in different surface forms (uppercase vs lowercase hex) do not trip spurious BridgeUnitDivergent mismatches.
deliverables: `BridgeUnitIdNormalisation` enum with `Exact` (default) and at least one folding variant
Symmetric application in the per-event comparison loop
acceptanceCriteria: `LowercaseHex` folds both sides before comparison; UUIDv7 inputs with case-only differences match.
The new option is plumbed through the fixture loader (`TraceFixtureOptions`).
auditFocus: Wire-shape impact on the fixture JSON (additive only)

findings.title: Branch Pass evidence reuses branch_id as bridge_unit_id placeholder (worker pre-identified)
findings.description: Worker-pre-identified deviation. Plan §4.3 specifies that branch-conformance Pass emits `one EvidenceRef::BridgeUnit per branch (the choice-index path's terminal branch unit)`. The implementation today reuses `GoldenBranch.branch_id` as the `bridge_unit_id` payload (`EvidenceRef::BridgeUnit { bridge_unit_id: branch.branch_id.clone() }`). This is semantically a placeholder: the `branch_id` is the discovery key, not the terminal bridge unit the choice-index path resolves to. The deviation is intentional and labeled by the worker as "UTSUSHI-031+ can refine" — the bridge-unit-id substrate (UTSUSHI-022 `TextLine.bridge_ref.bridge_unit_id`) is not joinable from a branch's `choice_index_path` without an engine adapter, which this slice explicitly does not ship. The evidence-ref payload validates against UTSUSHI-026's schema because `bridge_unit_id` is just a non-empty string; ingestion (UTSUSHI-030) joins by string equality, so the placeholder will round-trip safely until a real engine port lands.

evidence.detail: Plan §4.3 calls for `the choice-index path's terminal branch unit`, not the branch id.
Plan §14 / worker report flag this as a UTSUSHI-031+ refinement.

findings.impact: Today: zero — there is no engine-port consumer, ingestion validates the payload by shape only, and `branch_id` is itself a stable id. Once UTSUSHI-031+ engine ports emit real branches that resolve to terminal bridge units distinct from their discovery ids, the placeholder either needs an engine-side mapping (terminate-bridge-unit-id resolver) or the `BranchConformanceCheck` needs a typed `terminal_bridge_unit_id` field on `GoldenBranch` / `ObservedBranch`. The mis-labelled evidence-ref does NOT cause a false Pass — pairing semantics are id-keyed on `branch_id`, not on the evidence ref.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: `GoldenBranch` and `ObservedBranch` gain a `terminal_bridge_unit_id: String` field (required, non-empty, no whitespace, no local-path leak per the shared `validate_id_field`).
`build_branch_evidence` emits `EvidenceRef::BridgeUnit { bridge_unit_id: branch.terminal_bridge_unit_id.clone() }` instead of the branch id.
Fixture corpus is updated to add `terminalBridgeUnitId` to every golden / observed branch; ingestion (UTSUSHI-030) round-trip parity is re-verified.
Existing tests that previously asserted `bridge_unit_id == branch_id` are rewritten to assert against `terminal_bridge_unit_id`.

proposedDagNode.title: Real terminal-bridge-unit-id evidence on branch conformance Pass
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace the UTSUSHI-027 placeholder where branch-conformance Pass evidence cites `branch_id` as a `bridge_unit_id` with a real `terminal_bridge_unit_id` field on `GoldenBranch` / `ObservedBranch`, so ingestion can join discovered branches to their terminal bridge units without inferring identity from the discovery key.
deliverables: `terminal_bridge_unit_id: String` field on both golden and observed branch types
Updated `build_branch_evidence` to cite the new field
Fixture corpus updated (additive `terminalBridgeUnitId` on every branch)
Tests asserting the evidence ref carries the terminal id, not the branch id
acceptanceCriteria: The new field is required at construction with the shared id-validation rules.
`BranchCheckResult::Pass.evidence_refs` carries `terminal_bridge_unit_id` values that are distinct from `branch_id` in at least one positive fixture.
UTSUSHI-030 round-trip parity (when it lands) sees the new field through the JSON wire shape.
auditFocus: Schema impact (additive field, no version bump)
Test coverage for the distinct-id case

findings.title: into_conformance_result evidence-ref rejection surfaces MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE, not TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM
findings.description: Plan §6 / §9 specify that the trace-specific `TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM` code surfaces earlier than UTSUSHI-026's generic schema-level `evidence_tier_above_profile_ceiling` so reviewers can attribute the failure to the trace/branch check rather than the validator. The implementation routes the failure through `Err(ConformanceError::MalformedSemanticCode { code: codes::TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM.to_string() })` — but `ConformanceError::semantic_code()` returns `codes::MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE` for this variant (diagnostics.rs:197), so any downstream consumer that calls `error.semantic_code()` to triage the failure gets `utsushi.conformance.malformed_semantic_code`, not the trace overclaim code. The embedded `code` string is only visible via the `Display` impl. This subtly defeats the audit-focus surface: a reviewer triaging by stable code sees the generic malformed-code diagnostic and has to read the Display message to discover the trace-specific intent. The error variant being abused is also semantically wrong — `MalformedSemanticCode` was designed to signal that an INPUT code string (e.g. from a downstream provider) failed the `is_valid_semantic_code` shape check, not that an evidence-ref was inadmissible. The four call sites (trace.rs lines 340-342, 353-355; branch.rs lines 279-281, 292-294) all reuse this pattern; fixing one without the others would leave the surface inconsistent.

evidence.detail: Trace Pass-path evidence-ref rejection routes through MalformedSemanticCode with the overclaim code embedded.
ConformanceError::semantic_code() returns codes::MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE for the MalformedSemanticCode variant, ignoring the embedded code string.
Plan §6 commits to the trace-specific code surfacing 'earlier and points reviewers at the trace check rather than the generic schema validator'.
Plan §9.1 reaffirms the 'trace helper rejects on a tier-overclaim attempt with a specific code'.

findings.impact: Today there are no downstream code-based triagers, so the misrouting is invisible at runtime. The audit-focus surface ("Trace-only evidence overclaiming rendered playback") is partially defended: the helper DOES reject the offending evidence-ref at construction time (a real-world overclaim is caught and returned as Err), but the diagnostic the auditor receives identifies the generic malformed-code path rather than the trace overclaim. Reviewers writing future code that branches on `error.semantic_code()` will see one bucket where the plan promised two. Severity stays P3 because the rejection itself still happens — only the diagnostic surface is muddied.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: A new `ConformanceError::EvidenceRefNotAdmissibleForProfile { profile: ProfileId, evidence: &'static str }` variant (or equivalent) is added so the rejection has a typed home that is NOT `MalformedSemanticCode`.
`ConformanceError::semantic_code()` maps the new variant to `codes::TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM` (or a fresh `EVIDENCE_REF_NOT_ADMISSIBLE` if a per-profile breakout is preferred).
Both `TraceCheckResult::into_conformance_result` and `BranchCheckResult::into_conformance_result` are updated to return the new variant at all four call sites (trace.rs:340, 353; branch.rs:279, 292).
A test asserts `err.semantic_code() == codes::TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM` (or the breakout code) when a FrameArtifactRef is supplied to either helper.

proposedDagNode.title: Typed ConformanceError for inadmissible evidence-refs on trace and branch conformance
proposedDagNode.summary: Replace the four call sites in `trace_branch::{trace,branch}::into_conformance_result` that emit `Err(MalformedSemanticCode { code: TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM })` with a typed `ConformanceError::EvidenceRefNotAdmissibleForProfile` variant whose `semantic_code()` returns the trace-specific overclaim code, so downstream code-based triagers see the diagnostic the plan promised instead of the generic malformed-code bucket.
deliverables: All four call sites updated in trace.rs / branch.rs
Tests covering `.semantic_code()` for the rejection path on both Pass and Fail input to both helpers
acceptanceCriteria: Calling `TraceCheckResult::Pass { evidence_refs: vec![FrameArtifactRef{..}] }.into_conformance_result(...)` returns an Err whose `.semantic_code()` is `TRACE_EVIDENCE_TIER_OVERCLAIM`, not `MALFORMED_SEMANTIC_CODE`.
The same is true for the branch helper.
`MalformedSemanticCode` is no longer used as a carrier for evidence-ref rejections; it goes back to signalling input-code-shape failures only.
auditFocus: Backward-compatibility of MalformedSemanticCode for its original purpose
Symmetry across the four call sites
